div.font:
Steps
(1) The workload performs a DNS lookup for the AWS service domain (for example ssm.{region}.amazonaws.com). The VPC Route 53 Resolver checks the associated Private Hosted Zone.
(2) Because PrivateDnsEnabled created the Lattice-managed PHZ on association, the resolver returns an IP address managed by VPC Lattice (not part of the VPC CIDRs).
(3) The client connects on TCP 443 to the VPC Lattice IP. The service network does not terminate TLS; it forwards the raw TCP flow to the Resource Configuration's resource gateway.
(4) The resource gateway performs a DNS lookup on the configured VPC endpoint domain (vpce-{id}.{service}.{region}.vpce.amazonaws.com) and forwards the traffic. The source IP seen by the endpoint is the resource gateway's.
(5) The interface VPC endpoint (PrivateLink) forwards the traffic privately to the AWS service. The response returns along the same path.
